design_tone: calm, trusted, medical, clean, professional, patient-friendly
logo_element: Teal circle with white plus (+) cross medical icon

pages.name: Login Page
pages.design_note: Balanced split composition. Form panel left (~45%), illustration right (~55%). Clean card with rounded corners.

pages.name: Welcome Page
pages.design_note: Warm, guided landing. Illustration right of hero area. 3 secondary info cards below.

pages.name: Main Dashboard
components: 4 metric cards (Total, Scheduled, Completed, Prescriptions Available)
4 quick action cards (Book New, My Consultations, View Prescriptions, Contact Support)
pages.design_note: Portal dashboard with numeric metric cards in a 4-column row. Action cards in 2x2 grid below.

pages.name: Book Consultation
pages.form_layout: 2-column grid for top 8 fields, full-width textarea for symptoms
pages.design_note: Clean form inside wide card. 4 fields per row x 2 rows, then symptoms full-width.

pages.name: My Consultations
components: Consultation cards with: patient name, department, doctor, mode, date/time, status badge, Update button
pages.design_note: Data-dense list. Filter row pinned above card list. Status badge left of Update button.

pages.name: Prescriptions & Medical Summary
components: Prescription Status badge (Available for Completed / Pending otherwise)
pages.design_note: Card-based summary layout. Demo disclaimer prominent.

pages.name: Profile / Sign Out
pages.design_note: Single centered card with confirmation flow.

accessibility_requirements: All input fields must have visible labels
Buttons must have clear action names
Search and filters must be easy to find
Password input must use masked type
Support Playwright tests using accessible locators
visual_quality_baseline: Professional mockups already show Next.js/React-level quality with sidebar nav, metric cards, status badges, and proper desktop layouts. BRD technical stack (Streamlit) does not match the visual fidelity of the embedded mockups.
brd_stack_conflict_reason: BRD Section 14 requests Streamlit. However, the embedded mockups (Figures 1–8) depict a sidebar-nav web application layout with metric cards, grid forms, status badges, and split-screen login that is consistent with a React/Next.js production web app, not a Streamlit prototype.
